_doc: Foundry closing-sweep verdicts (Round 88) -- exhaust the enumerable frame architectures for the d11-605 / d12-842 kissing records.
adjudicator: arena/workspace/kissing_frame_adjudicate.py (ring Q(sqrt d) generalized; certified-theta KILL; joint mode; intra-cell assertion; --selftest PASS)

B2r.note: genuinely Q(sqrt2)-irrational hybrids; rotation destroys integer weight-4 richness

B3.note: PURE Q(sqrt5) sector (axes+icosa+weight-4 base); sqrt2 hybrids are biquadratic Q(sqrt2,sqrt5) -> tool-blocked

B5.family: Steiner S(4,5,11) weight-5 world, M11-symmetric, k=0

B7.note: steepest frame gain (net+15) but thinnest base weight-4 world (15 cells)

K5.note: pool is an induced subgraph of K2 grand-mixed (A u W4 u U_[8]); MIS(K5) <= MIS(K2)

B10_negative_theorem: INCONCLUSIVE-as-theorem / EXHAUSTED-by-enumeration: rigorous whole-cell ceiling 291, Bianchi extremum 302 (proven), and every frame-upgrade family theta-closed <= 126 this sweep. The general negative theorem remains formally open (needs a rigorous distinct-damage floor); no realized pattern >= 303 exists across the enumerable space.

B10_rigorous_ceilings.D_11_4_3_status: exact (max partial-Steiner packing of 4-subsets of [11], every triple <= once); verified HiGHS in foundry_wave2/B10 (scipy_status 0).
B10_rigorous_ceilings.weight4_world_MIS_note: = 8 * D(11,4,3); rigorous upper on alpha_exp for ANY extension (exposure only removes weight-4 vertices, MIS is monotone).
B10_rigorous_ceilings.bianchi_extremum_status: RIGOROUS max over ALL Bianchi 1-factor / pair-system frames (foundry_wave2 B1: max_TOTAL_upper = 302, all_closed_rigorous = true).

campaign_conclusion: EXHAUSTED. Every enumerable frame architecture we can currently write down is CLOSED far below target (d11 frames cap <= 126 lines vs 303; d12 8+4 modular = 324 vs 421). Combined with the prior-wave closures (B1=302, B6=267, K1=359, K2=420, B2-unrotated=299), the weight-4-exposure + frame-extension + split-modular design space is sealed for every method we can bring. REMAINS OPEN: (i) the B3 sqrt2/sqrt5 BIQUADRATIC hybrid sector (needs Q(sqrt2,sqrt5) ring in the tool); (ii) a formal closed-form negative theorem (B10) -- exhausted empirically, not proven.

runtimes_approx.B2r: theta SDP ~15 min (803 v); KaMIS 87 in 240s; HiGHS bracket 400s
runtimes_approx.B7: theta 80.76 ~1 min; HiGHS exact 70 <2 min (746 v)
